name: ServiceNow, Inc.

profile.description: ServiceNow, Inc. is the leading enterprise AI workflow platform, orchestrating digital workflows across IT, HR, customer service, security, and finance for over 8,100 customers including 85% of the Fortune 500. Under CEO Bill McDermott, the company has transformed from an IT ticketing tool into a 'platform of platforms' that connects AI agents across enterprise operations. FY2025 revenue was $13.3 billion with 22% subscription revenue growth. Headquartered in Santa Clara, California.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): ServiceNow has executed one of enterprise software's most successful platform pivots — transforming from an ITSM ticketing system into an enterprise AI workflow orchestration platform, expanding TAM from approximately $20B to $220B+ [1]. Q1 2026 subscription revenue of $3.67B growing 22% YoY demonstrates sustained execution on this transformation. AI Deployment (2/2): AI agents are shipping to customers with measurable results — ServiceNow's Now Assist generative AI features are deployed across the platform, and the Moveworks acquisition adds AI-powered customer service capabilities [2]. CEO McDermott describes ServiceNow as the 'AI control tower for business reinvention' with specific agentic AI features in production. Market Share (1/2): Category leader in enterprise workflow automation with no close independent competitor; 85% Fortune 500 penetration and net revenue retention consistently above 125% demonstrate platform dominance [1]. Product Pipeline (1/2): Armis cybersecurity acquisition expected to close H2 2026, expanding into security operations TAM; agentic AI features and multi-agent orchestration capabilities are named products with specific timelines [2]. R&D Intensity (1/1): R&D spending of approximately $2.8B (21% of revenue) is above enterprise software peers, reflecting investment in platform AI capabilities and the Now Platform architecture.

inspiration.label: STRONG
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): ServiceNow's purpose — 'making the world of work, work better for people' — is specific, service-oriented, and connects enterprise technology to a genuine human benefit: freeing workers from repetitive tasks to focus on meaningful work [2]. McDermott has articulated this vision consistently since joining in 2019, and the phrase 'people before profits' is a recurring leadership theme across his tenure at SAP and ServiceNow. Community Impact (1/2): ServiceNow.org provides platform access to nonprofit organizations, and the company has committed to 100% renewable electricity and carbon neutrality by 2030 with SBTi-approved targets [3]. General corporate philanthropy rather than distinctive community investment. Employee Retention (1/2): McDermott has built one of the strongest corporate cultures in enterprise tech, with consistently high employee engagement scores and above-average retention for the software industry — the 'hungry and humble' culture is widely cited as a competitive advantage [2]. Insider Buying (2/2): CEO McDermott purchased $3M of NOW shares in open-market transactions during the January 2026 software sector sell-off, demonstrating maximum personal financial conviction at a moment of market uncertainty [2].

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics. Q1 2026 delivered $3.77B total revenue growing 22% YoY, with subscription revenue of $3.67B growing 22% YoY and FY2026 guidance of $15.74-15.78B subscription revenue (22-22.5% growth) [1]. D/E of 20.7% is well below the 70% target. ROE of 16.1% exceeds the 15% target. Revenue growth of 22.1% YoY and 22.4% 3-year CAGR both exceed the 10% target significantly. Forward P/E of 24.7x is above but close to the 20x target, scoring 0.88. Profit margin of 12.6% is below the 20% target but improving with operating leverage. Forward PEG of 1.21 is near the 1.0 target, scoring 0.90 [4]. The strong growth profile and clean balance sheet offset the margin and valuation premium.

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (5/9): ServiceNow's core thesis is that AI creates more demand for enterprise workflow orchestration, not less — McDermott argues 'AI excels at identifying problems but cannot execute solutions,' positioning ServiceNow as the execution layer [2]. This is compelling but carries genuine uncertainty: if agentic AI systems mature to orchestrate enterprise workflows autonomously, the 'execution layer' could be bypassed. JPMorgan has flagged ServiceNow as AI-resilient, but the stock's 10%+ decline during the January 2026 software sector sell-off shows the market has not fully resolved this question. Deep enterprise integration (85% Fortune 500, 125%+ NRR) creates switching costs that protect the installed base. AI Opportunity (1/1): ServiceNow generates quantifiable AI revenue through Now Assist generative AI features sold across the platform, Moveworks AI customer service contributing approximately 1% of revenue, and AI-driven workflow automation that drives upsell to higher-tier pricing [1][2].

infinite_game.summary: Bill McDermott operates with genuine infinite-game commitment — his 'people before profits' philosophy, $3M personal share purchase during a sector sell-off, contract extension through 2030, and consistent refusal to sacrifice long-term platform integrity for short-term revenue optimization reflect a leader playing to keep playing. ServiceNow's transformation from ITSM ticketing to enterprise AI platform demonstrates the kind of existential flexibility that Sinek celebrates.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): 'Making the world of work, work better for people' is genuine, service-oriented, specific, and invites both customers and employees to participate in building a future with less workplace friction [2]. McDermott has articulated this consistently since 2019 across multiple economic cycles. Strategic decisions — the Moveworks and Armis acquisitions, the AI agent architecture, the 'platform of platforms' strategy — clearly trace back to this purpose. Not yet a 3 because the cause hasn't survived a leadership transition or multi-decade crisis.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (2/2): McDermott has built one of the strongest cultures in enterprise tech — the 'hungry and humble' ethos is widely cited by employees and analysts as a competitive advantage [2]. Leadership stability is exceptional with McDermott extending through 2030. The company's framing of AI as amplifying employees rather than replacing them creates psychological safety. Employee engagement scores consistently rank in the top tier for enterprise software.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): McDermott explicitly acknowledges Salesforce, SAP (his former employer), and Microsoft as rivals whose platform capabilities push ServiceNow to develop better integrations and expand [2]. The Moveworks and Armis acquisitions reflect competitive awareness — recognizing gaps in AI customer service and security that rivals' strengths revealed. Competitive language focuses on building the best platform, not defeating competitors.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (2/2): ServiceNow's transformation from ITSM ticketing tool (2004-2015) to enterprise workflow platform (2015-2020) to enterprise AI platform (2020-present) represents textbook existential flexibility [2]. Each pivot built on the previous while expanding TAM by an order of magnitude. The company chose to redefine its category rather than defend its ITSM market share — precisely what Sinek describes.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): McDermott's $3M personal share purchase during the January 2026 software sector rout demonstrates acting in service of conviction at personal financial cost [2]. The Rule of 55+ standard (revenue growth + FCF margin > 55%) maintains long-term capital discipline. However, given ServiceNow's $128B market cap and consistent 22% growth, these decisions carry less relative risk than a true bet-the-company move would.

investment_thesis: ServiceNow is the enterprise AI workflow platform that benefits from — rather than is threatened by — the AI revolution. Q1 2026 delivered $3.77B revenue growing 22% YoY, with FY2026 subscription revenue guidance of $15.74-15.78B representing 22-22.5% growth [1]. The company combines rare attributes: $14B+ revenue scale with 22% growth, improving operating margins, $5.1B in free cash flow, 85% Fortune 500 penetration, and 125%+ net revenue retention. CEO McDermott's $3M personal share purchase, 2030 contract extension, and 'people before profits' philosophy signal maximum long-term conviction [2]. The Armis acquisition (H2 2026 close) expands the security TAM, and agentic AI features are generating measurable customer adoption.
thesis_continued: The primary risk is valuation: at 24.7x forward earnings and 74x trailing P/E, ServiceNow is priced for sustained 20%+ growth for years. The stock's 10%+ decline during the January 2026 software sector sell-off exposed genuine market anxiety about whether agentic AI systems could eventually bypass enterprise workflow platforms — the 'execution layer' thesis is compelling but unproven against rapidly evolving AI capabilities [2]. Profit margin of 12.6% is below software peers despite 76.6% gross margins, reflecting heavy investment in sales and R&D. Forward PEG of 1.21 is reasonable for the growth rate, but any deceleration below 20% subscription growth could trigger significant multiple compression. The company does not pay a dividend, returning no capital to shareholders despite strong free cash flow generation.
key_catalysts: Armis cybersecurity acquisition closing in H2 2026 expands platform TAM into security operations and adds approximately 1% incremental revenue contribution
Agentic AI adoption metrics in Q2-Q3 2026 results showing specific enterprise workflow automation data and customer ROI would validate the 'AI control tower' thesis
Subscription revenue growth sustaining above 22% through FY2026 at $15.7B+ scale would justify the premium valuation and demonstrate durable platform momentum
key_risks: Agentic AI systems maturing to autonomously orchestrate enterprise workflows within 2-3 years could bypass ServiceNow's 'execution layer,' undermining the core value proposition
Forward P/E of 24.7x and trailing P/E of 74x leave minimal margin of safety — any subscription growth deceleration below 20% could trigger significant multiple compression
GAAP profit margin of 12.6% despite 76.6% gross margins reflects heavy sales and R&D investment that must translate to operating leverage or the profitability thesis weakens
sources: ServiceNow Q1 2026 and FY2025 Financial Results — SEC 8-K Filings, January and April 2026
Bill McDermott Leadership, AI Strategy, and Share Purchase — BBN Times, LEADERS Magazine, StartupHub.ai, 2025-2026
ServiceNow ESG Commitments and Net Zero Targets — servicenow.com, SEC DEF 14A Proxy Statement, 2025-2026
Yahoo Finance — NOW Key Statistics and Financial Data, accessed May 2026
